title.zh: Pillar Two 從規則建構轉向行政簡化：Side-by-Side 配套與其未解問題
title.en: Pillar Two shifts from rule-building to administrative simplification — and its unresolved edges

summary.zh: 2026 年最重要的三份文件已全部完成研讀：Consolidated Commentary（456 頁，2026-05-11 核定）、Global Minimum Tax Implementation Toolkit（97 頁，2026-04-22 核定，聚焦 Orbis 資料、CbC 申報、GIR 交換機制）、Side-by-Side Package（88 頁，2026-01-05 核定）。Side-by-Side Package 的核心是讓母公司所在地已有 Qualified SbS Regime 的跨國集團，可就全球所有營運選擇適用零補足稅——但截至 2026 年 1 月僅有美國被列為合格，且該資格繫於美國國會維持 CAMT 並不大幅調降企業稅率這個政治前提，本身即是制度不確定性的來源。安全港機制本身也非萬無一失：OECD Central Record 將 Qatar 的 QDMTT 生效日期列為 2024-01-01，但 Qatar 官方公告、HMRC 參考清單與 Side-by-Side Package Notice 2 附表均記載為 2025-01-01——同一套「已確認合格」的規則，仍存在跨文件版本的日期落差。
summary.en: All three key 2026 documents have been read in full: the Consolidated Commentary (456 pp., approved 2026-05-11), the Global Minimum Tax Implementation Toolkit (97 pp., approved 2026-04-22, focused on Orbis data sourcing, CbC reporting, and GIR exchange mechanics), and the Side-by-Side Package (88 pp., approved 2026-01-05). The Side-by-Side Package's core mechanism lets an MNE group headquartered in a jurisdiction with a Qualified SbS Regime elect a deemed zero top-up tax across all worldwide operations — but as of January 2026 only the United States has that status, and its eligibility is itself contingent on Congress retaining CAMT and not materially cutting the corporate rate, making the safe harbour's own stability a live political question. The safe harbours aren't error-free either: the OECD Central Record lists Qatar's QDMTT as effective 2024-01-01, while Qatar's own domestic announcements, HMRC's reference list, and the Side-by-Side Package's own Notice 2 table all say 2025-01-01 — a documented date discrepancy inside a rule already marked 'qualified.'

zh: 補上 Simplified ETR / 實質本位租稅優惠安全港的條文層級摘要
以 OECD Working Paper No. 76 的 EATR 與治理結果，比較所得型與支出型優惠在 GloBE 下的待遇；另區分經濟效果、GloBE 分類與救濟可預測性
追蹤是否有第二個 Qualified SbS Regime 出現（目前僅美國），檢驗此制度是否只是美國專用安排
核對 Qatar QDMTT 日期落差是否已在 OECD 下一輪 Central Record 更新中修正
en: Add an article-level summary of the Simplified ETR and Substance-based Tax Incentive safe harbours

Use OECD Working Paper No. 76's EATR and governance findings to compare income- and expenditure-based incentives under GloBE, while separating economic effect, GloBE classification, and remedial predictability
Watch for a second jurisdiction to obtain Qualified SbS Regime status (currently US-only) to test whether this is a US-specific arrangement in practice
Check whether the Qatar QDMTT date discrepancy gets corrected in the next OECD Central Record update

title.zh: UN 稅務合作框架公約與國際稅務治理重心移動：一個資料驗證缺口
title.en: The UN tax convention and the shift in international tax governance — with a real verification gap

summary.zh: UN 路線的研究價值在於制度正當性、發展中國家參與、徵稅權配置。談判座標已可由一手文本核實：INC 第四輪於 2026 年 2 月結束，INC-5 2026-08-03–13 在紐約、INC-6 2026-11-30–12-11 在奈洛比舉行，公約與兩項自選早期議定書（跨境服務所得課稅、稅務爭端之預防與解決）預定 2027 年下半年提交聯大第 82 屆會議（CRP.28 §1）；兩項議定書定位為可單獨簽署的自選條約（UN DESA 閉幕致詞頁，2025-08-15）。三份工作流一手文件（CRP.26 框架公約模板 2026-01-22、CRP.27 服務課稅選項文件 2026-01-21、CRP.28 爭端預防與解決概念說明 2026-01-23）互為印證同一治理缺口：框架公約本身在爭端解決上完全空白（第 13 條「待補」，僅指向 WS3 議定書；第 22 條一般爭端解決條款同樣「待補」），且第 21 條第 4 項明定公約締約國無須加入議定書即可承擔實體協調義務——這是「協調而不連帶課予救濟義務」的條約結構性證據，直接對應研究問題的核心假設。CRP.28 §12 的「無自動優先效力」預設，使 WS3 議定書即便完成草擬，也只是與既有雙邊 MAP 條款、投資仲裁並存的第三層，而非取代或整合它們。§41 仲裁設計為任擇、最後手段、僅限主管機關間、無先例效力、且明文排除納稅人為程序當事人的 final-offer 制——與 OECD 式強制拘束仲裁形成鮮明對照，是「較弱救濟層」論證最具體的一手文本證據；MAP 條款以 UN 範本第 25 條為起點，明列取得 MAP 不得受國內程序不當限縮（§37），與 Danon 的 MAP 取得批評直接相接。CRP.26 第 5 條含重談不一致既有稅約的義務；CRP.27 §42 顯示 WS1（第 5 條重談義務）與 WS2（Article 5/7 覆蓋既有條約）在稅約覆寫機制上平行但獨立起草，兩者均未指向具體救濟途徑——WS2 §43 明確將爭端解決全數留給 WS3。CRP.26 第 6 條 HNWI 條款為行為義務（偵測、資訊分享、探索協調做法），未達 GloBE 式量化義務的拘束力，其違反較可能落入第 9–10 條行政互助軌道而非未來 WS3 爭端機制。爭端議定書工作流與 OECD 路線的分岔已有定調跡象：TJN 主張不納入 OECD 式 MAP 強制拘束仲裁，仲裁至少應為自選且非強制，並提議 MAP 作為稅務相關投資爭端的排他管道（TJN 第四輪後意見書，2026-04-03）。另一條獨立線索是 UN 範本稅約 2025 年 3 月新增、意圖阻斷投資人於稅務相關爭議中援引 ISDS 的條款：Danon 與 Martín Jiménez（2025-04-15）批評其「整體政府」理論站不住腳，條款具自我判斷性質而可能被仲裁庭以越權為由拒絕承認其管轄限制效力，也未處理與《能源憲章條約》等多邊協定的相容性；該條款屬另一份文件，與框架公約維持分開分析。
summary.en: The UN track matters for legitimacy, developing-country participation, and the allocation of taxing rights. The negotiation coordinates are verifiable from primary text: the fourth INC session concluded in February 2026, INC-5 runs 3–13 Aug 2026 (NY) and INC-6 30 Nov–11 Dec 2026 (Nairobi), with the Convention and the two optional early protocols (cross-border services income; prevention and resolution of tax disputes) due at the UNGA 82nd session in H2 2027 (CRP.28 §1); both protocols are framed as optional, stand-alone treaties (UN DESA closing-remarks page, 2025-08-15). The three workstream primary papers (CRP.26 Framework Convention Template 2026-01-22, CRP.27 Services Taxation Options Paper 2026-01-21, CRP.28 Dispute Prevention and Resolution Concept Note 2026-01-23) corroborate the same governance gap from three angles: the Framework Convention itself is entirely silent on dispute resolution (Article 13 is "to come" and points only to the WS3 protocol; Article 22, the general Convention-dispute clause, is likewise undrafted), and Article 21(4) establishes that Convention Parties incur the substantive coordination obligations without any requirement to also join the dispute-resolution protocol — direct treaty-structural evidence for "coordination without a correspondingly mandatory remedial layer," the core premise of the research question. CRP.28 §12's "no automatic superseding effect" default means that even a fully drafted WS3 protocol would sit as a third, non-displacing layer alongside existing bilateral MAP clauses and investment arbitration rather than consolidating them. §41's arbitration design — optional, last-resort, competent-authority-only, no precedential value, and expressly excluding taxpayers as parties, on a final-offer basis — contrasts sharply with OECD-style mandatory binding MAP arbitration and is the most concrete primary-text evidence for the "thinner remedial layer" argument; the MAP provision starts from the UN Model's MAP article with an express requirement that access to MAP not be unduly restricted by domestic procedural requirements (§37), connecting directly to Danon's MAP-access critique. CRP.26's Article 5 includes an obligation to renegotiate inconsistent existing treaties; CRP.27 §42 shows WS1 (Article 5's renegotiation obligation) and WS2 (overriding Articles 5/7 of existing treaties) pursuing parallel but independently drafted treaty-override mechanisms, neither of which specifies a remedy for non-compliance — WS2 §43 explicitly defers all dispute-resolution content to WS3. CRP.26 Article 6's HNWI clause imposes conduct obligations (detect, share information, explore coordination) rather than GloBE-style quantified obligations, and a breach is more likely to surface through the Articles 9-10 mutual-assistance track than through any future WS3 dispute mechanism. The dispute-protocol workstream shows a divergence from the OECD path: TJN argues against OECD-style mandatory binding MAP arbitration — arbitration should at a minimum be optional and non-mandatory — and proposes MAP as the exclusive venue for tax-related investment disputes (TJN post-fourth-session submission, 2026-04-03). A separate strand is the UN Model Tax Convention clause added in March 2025 intended to block investors from invoking ISDS in tax-related disputes: Danon and Martín Jiménez (2025-04-15) argue its 'whole of government' rationale doesn't hold up, that the clause is self-judging and may be rejected by tribunals as an ultra vires jurisdictional limit, and that it leaves compatibility with multilateral instruments like the Energy Charter Treaty unaddressed; that clause is a separate instrument and stays analytically distinct from the Framework Convention.

zh: INC-5（2026-08-03–13）前後追蹤三工作流零草案：CRP.28 §3 預告 WS3 議定書零草案將提交第五屆會議，發布即研讀
對 CRP.26/27/28 做逐段摘要，特別是 CRP.26 第 5 條（課稅權公平分配含重談既有稅約義務）與第 6 條（HNWI）、CRP.28 §37 的 MAP 取得保障
區分框架公約與 ISDS override 條款的法律位階——兩者不是同一份文件
en: Track the three workstreams' zero drafts around INC-5 (3–13 Aug 2026): CRP.28 §3 announces a WS3 protocol zero draft for the fifth session — download and parse on release
Do a paragraph-level digest of CRP.26/27/28 — especially CRP.26 Articles 5 (fair allocation incl. renegotiation obligation) and 6 (HNWI), and CRP.28 §37's MAP-access safeguards
Keep the framework convention and the ISDS-override clause analytically separate — they are not the same document

title.zh: Danon/洛桑研究線：協調化稅法規則的救濟確定性
title.en: Danon/Lausanne: remedial certainty for coordinated tax rules

summary.zh: 這條研究線已收斂為一個 PhD 申請問題：當 GloBE 這類協調化稅法規則透過各國國內法實施，而爭端可能分流到國內救濟、MAP、OECD 範本第 25(3) 條主管機關諮商、稅約仲裁或投資仲裁時，國際稅法如何維持「救濟確定性」？Danon 與 Gutmann、Maisto、Martín Jiménez 的 2022/2023 GloBE 文章提供法學核心：2022 年文章以 Article 25(3)、互惠原則、國內互惠爭端解決條款與 MAAC 建立沒有 Pillar Two 多邊公約時的可行橋樑；2023 年文章進一步區分 conflicts of interpretation 與 conflicts of transposition，提出 interpretative model rule、lex specialis rule、domestic dispute-resolution rule 與 adjustment rule，以回應 legality objection。Danon/Wuschka 2021 投資協定文章則不是第二個取代性主軸，而是 boundary test：它區分 tax dispute proper 與 tax-related investment dispute，說明 MAP 作為國家對國家程序的限制，以及 FET、tax carve-outs、systemic integration 在稅收措施影響投資人時如何成為外部救濟邊界。MAP access 批判、UN/OECD 制度設計與 Manus-Meta 技術交易案例，均應服務此核心問題；Manus-Meta 只作壓力測試，不作論文本體。
summary.en: This line has been narrowed into a PhD-application research problem: how can international tax law secure remedial certainty when coordinated tax rules such as GloBE are implemented through domestic law, while disputes may be routed through domestic remedies, MAP, Article 25(3) competent-authority consultation, tax-treaty arbitration, or investment arbitration? Danon, Gutmann, Maisto, and Martín Jiménez's 2022/2023 GloBE articles supply the doctrinal core. The 2022 article builds a workable bridge through Article 25(3), reciprocity, reciprocal domestic dispute-resolution provisions, and the MAAC in the absence of a Pillar Two multilateral convention. The 2023 article adds the legal architecture: conflicts of interpretation, conflicts of transposition, an interpretative model rule, a lex specialis rule, a domestic dispute-resolution rule, and an adjustment rule designed to answer the legality objection. Danon/Wuschka 2021 should not displace that core; it functions as a boundary test. It distinguishes a tax dispute proper from a tax-related investment dispute, shows the limits of MAP as a state-to-state mechanism, and explains how FET, tax carve-outs, and systemic integration may become relevant when fiscal measures affect protected investors. MAP access, UN/OECD dispute design, and the Manus-Meta technology transaction should all serve this core problem; Manus-Meta remains a stress test, not the thesis object.

zh: 把英文 contact proposal 壓成 1–2 頁寄信用版本，保留 remedial certainty、legality objection、tax dispute proper / tax-related investment dispute 三個核心術語
將 UN/OECD 制度比較接到 GloBE remedial certainty：CRP.28 optional arbitration、MAP access safeguards、competent-authority design 是否解決同一個問題
用 2017/2020 PPT 文章校正 Manus-Meta 的 treaty abuse 分析，避免把 PPT 寫成一般實質課稅規則
en: Compress the English contact proposal into a 1-2 page version while preserving the three core terms: remedial certainty, legality objection, and tax dispute proper / tax-related investment dispute

Connect the UN/OECD institutional comparison back to GloBE remedial certainty: CRP.28 optional arbitration, MAP access safeguards, and competent-authority design should be tested against the same problem
Use the 2017/2020 PPT articles to discipline the Manus-Meta treaty-abuse analysis and avoid turning PPT into a general substance-over-form rule

title.zh: 高淨值個人、財富稅與全球稅逃漏資料：Zucman 團隊的 2024 年報告
title.en: High-net-worth individuals, wealth taxation, and global tax evasion data: the Zucman team's 2024 report

summary.zh: EU Tax Observatory《Global Tax Evasion Report 2024》（91 頁）由 Annette Alstadsæter、Sarah Godar、Panayiotis Nicolaides、Gabriel Zucman 統籌，Joseph Stiglitz 撰寫前言，分五章處理：離岸稅逃漏趨勢、企業利潤移轉趨勢、國際稅收競爭新形式、高淨值個人的稅收缺口、以及收取該缺口的政策選項。該機構另設一頁彙整五份具體估計研究：Pellegrini et al.（2016）、Vellutini et al.（2019）、Alstadsæter（2018）、ECORYS（2021）、Tax Justice Network 的《State of Tax Justice 2021》——涵蓋離岸財富規模、依所有權國分列的離岸財富、以及各國稅收流失估計。UN 談判與這份資料共同把高淨值個人有效課稅推成政策熱點，但方法論本身的限制（離岸財富的隱密性本質使精確估計困難）與資料可得性，仍是這個議題無法迴避的核心限制，而非附帶說明。UN 框架公約模板（CRP.26，2026-01-22）第 6 條即為 HNWI 條款：締約方「應發展並實施措施以偵測、嚇阻及防止高淨值個人之避稅與逃稅」——本主題自此有一手談判文本可引。
summary.en: The EU Tax Observatory's Global Tax Evasion Report 2024 (91 pp.), coordinated by Annette Alstadsæter, Sarah Godar, Panayiotis Nicolaides, and Gabriel Zucman with a foreword by Joseph Stiglitz, covers five chapters: trends in offshore tax evasion, trends in corporate profit shifting, new forms of international tax competition, tax deficits of high-net-worth individuals, and policy options for collecting that deficit. The Observatory maintains a separate page indexing five specific estimation studies — Pellegrini et al. (2016), Vellutini et al. (2019), Alstadsæter (2018), ECORYS (2021), and the Tax Justice Network's *State of Tax Justice 2021* — covering the scale of offshore wealth globally, by country of ownership, and estimated tax revenue losses by country. UN negotiations and this data together push effective HNWI taxation into policy focus, but the methodology's own limits (the inherently secretive nature of offshore wealth makes precise estimation genuinely hard) and data availability are the topic's central constraint, not a footnote to it. The UN Framework Convention template (CRP.26, 2026-01-22) makes HNWI its Article 6: States Parties 'shall develop and implement measures to detect, deter and prevent tax avoidance and evasion by high-net-worth individuals' — this topic now has primary negotiation text to cite.

zh: 補上五份底層研究的個別方法論摘要
整理離岸財富與高淨值個人課稅的可引用數據（報告第 1、4 章）
對照 CRP.26 第 6 條的 HNWI 行為義務措辭與 EU Tax Observatory 的量化主張，界定義務強度與可執行性的落差
en: Summarize each of the five underlying offshore-wealth studies' methodology individually
Extract citable figures on offshore wealth and HNWI taxation from the report's Chapters 1 and 4
Compare the conduct-obligation wording of CRP.26 Article 6 on HNWIs with the EU Tax Observatory's quantitative claims, to define the gap between duty strength and enforceability
